page: Vultr vhf-2c-4gb is a virtual server within the High Frequency Compute family, featuring 2 vCPUs and 4.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an x86_64 Intel Core Processor (Skylake architecture) running at 2.0 GHz, this instance utilizes a shared CPU allocation model with 2 physical cores and 1 thread per core. The hardware configuration includes a 16 MB L3 cache, 4 MB L2 cache, and 32 KB L1d cache, alongside support for AVX, AES, and BMI2 instruction sets. Local storage is bundled, providing 128 GB of NVMe SSD capacity, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate a mix of capabilities, with single-threaded operations showing the greatest relative strength. The instance achieves a strong single-threaded gzip compression rate of 32.63 GB/sec and an average stress-ng single-core CPU performance score of 1873.78. However, multi-core and memory-intensive tasks show limitations, with stress-ng multi-core performance (3596.49) and PassMark CPU score (4124.35) falling into the weaker percentiles. Memory write bandwidth for large blocks is poor at 12.89 GB/sec, and database operations score a low 999.52 on PassMark.

Given its shared CPU architecture and modest memory capacity, this instance offers a cost-effective entry point for resource-constrained workloads that benefit from fast NVMe storage but do not require sustained, dedicated multi-core processing. It is suitable for single-threaded utility tasks, light static web serving, basic Redis caching, and development or testing environments.
description: Vultr vhf-2c-4gb is a virtual server in the High Frequency Compute family, configured with 2 shared vCPUs on an Intel Skylake x86_64 architecture running at 2.0 GHz. It features 4.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 128 GB NVMe SSD. While single-threaded performance is relatively strong, as demonstrated by a gzip compression rate of 32.63 GB/sec, multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks are generally weak, with a PassMark CPU score of 4124.35 and large-block memory write speeds of 12.89 GB/sec. This resource profile makes the instance a cost-efficient option for lightweight, single-threaded workloads, basic web serving, and development environments that require fast local storage but can tolerate shared CPU limits.
og_description: Vultr vhf-2c-4gb is a High Frequency Compute instance featuring 2 shared Intel vCPUs, 4.0 GB RAM, and 128 GB NVMe SSD storage, offering strong single-threaded performance for lightweight workloads.

tagline: A budget-friendly shared virtual server featuring fast NVMe storage and solid single-threaded performance for lightweight utility workloads.
bullet_points: 2 shared Intel Skylake vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz
4.0 GB of system memory and 128 GB of bundled NVMe SSD storage
Strong single-threaded performance with 32.63 GB/sec gzip compression
Shared CPU allocation suited for burstable or non-continuous workloads
Best fit for light web serving, single-threaded utilities, and development
